name: Ridge
overview: Ridge is a small, dispersed hamlet in the civil parish of Chilmark, in the Nadder Valley of south-west Wiltshire, lying to the south-west of Chilmark village within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape. The Fonthill estate extends into the parish as far as Ridge. A small Baptist chapel (later the Union chapel) was built here between 1851 and 1864; by 2003 it had passed into private hands. Day-to-day amenities are found in Chilmark and, more fully, in nearby Tisbury.
character: A scattered, tranquil rural hamlet of farmsteads and cottages on the edge of the Fonthill estate, deeply rural and quiet.
amenities: Amenities in Chilmark village (pub, primary school, church, cricket club)
Fuller amenities in Tisbury (~4 miles): shops, station, surgeries, leisure centre
Surrounding Cranborne Chase and Fonthill estate walks

schools.name: Chilmark and Fonthill Bishop CofE Primary School (in Chilmark)
Secondary schools in the Tisbury/Wilton/Salisbury area

prices.summary: A scarce, very rural hamlet; mostly farmhouses and cottages on the edge of the Fonthill estate, rarely coming to market. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks on the Fonthill estate and through Cranborne Chase
Visiting Chilmark village and its pub
Nadder Valley countryside and quiet lanes
pros: Deeply rural and peaceful on the Fonthill estate edge
Chilmark's pub and school and Tisbury's amenities within reach
cons: A dispersed hamlet with effectively no amenities of its own
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a deeply rural, secluded hamlet within reach of Chilmark and Tisbury, who prioritise peace and countryside.
